This is a common pattern for module aggregation.\n- **Malicious or suspicious activity:** No executable code, no dynamic behavior, no input handling, no network communication.\n- **Source map comment:** Standard in development environments; no inherent security risk unless source maps are exposed in production, which is not indicated here.\n\n**Conclusion:** No issues are present in the code itself.\n\n---\n\n### Review of each report:\n\n#### Reports 1-3:\n- **Purpose & Sources:** Re-exporting modules, standard pattern.\n- **Sinks & Flows:** No data sinks or flows.\n- **Anomalies:** Presence of source map comment, which is typical.\n- **Analysis & Conclusion:** All reports correctly identify the code as boilerplate module re-exports with no malicious intent or security concerns.\n- **Confidence:** 1 (high confidence)\n- **Obfuscated:** 0\n- **Malware:** 0\n- **Security Risk:** 0\n\n**These reports are consistent and accurate.**\n\n---\n\n#### Report 4:\n- **Purpose & Sources:** Same as above.\n- **Sinks & Flows:** No data handling.\n- **Anomalies:** Source map comment noted, with a mention that in production, source maps could be a concern if exposed.\n- **Analysis & Conclusion:** Same as above, with a slight note on source maps in production. The security risk score is 0.1, which seems slightly elevated but still low, perhaps reflecting a minor concern about source map exposure.\n\n**Justification for securityRisk=0.1:**  \nWhile source maps are useful for debugging, exposing them in production can leak source code, which might be exploited or analyzed maliciously. However, since this code appears to be a simple re-export, and no other malicious activity is detected, a low risk score is appropriate.\n\n---\n\n#### Report 5:\n- **Purpose & Sources:** Same pattern.\n- **Sinks & Flows:** No untrusted data handling.\n- **Anomalies:** Source map comment, typical boilerplate.\n- **Analysis & Conclusion:** Confirmed as standard module re-export code with no security issues.\n\n**Overall, all reports agree that this code is benign and standard.**\n\n---\n\n### Critical evaluation of scores:\n\n- **Malware Score:** All reports assign 0, which is appropriate.
